name: Demo Charging (OCPP 1.6)
description: Self-contained OCPP 1.6 §4.9 connector lifecycle demo. Walks the connector through Available → Preparing → Charging → Finishing → Available, with a remote-start authorization step and a periodic Sample.Periodic MeterValue stream that stops when the EV reaches its target SoC. Use with --scenario-template-file to apply to every connector of a multi-connector CP.

data.label: Wait for BootNotification.conf
data.description: Give the CSMS time to Accept the BootNotification before exercising the connector. §4.2 — only Accepted boots may send StartTransaction.req.

data.label: EV plug in → Preparing
data.description: §4.9 A2: cable inserted, EV pilot detected. The connector advertises Preparing to the CSMS while it waits for an idTag / RemoteStart.

data.label: EV / cable handshake
data.description: ISO 15118 / cable continuity check on a real CP. We model it as a brief settle so Preparing is visible on the CSMS side.

data.label: Wait for RemoteStartTransaction
data.description: §5.11: CSMS authorises the session by sending RemoteStartTransaction.req. The forwarded idTag flows into the next Transaction node.

data.label: StartTransaction.req
data.description: §4.7 — sends StartTransaction.req with the idTag from RemoteStart. The domain transitions Preparing → Charging when the CSMS responds Accepted.

data.label: MeterValues (Sample.Periodic, stop at target SoC)
data.description: §5.9 — periodic MeterValues.req with Energy.Active.Import.Register. Increment matches an accelerated 150 kW DC fast-charge curve (1.25 kWh per 30 s). Stops automatically when the EV reaches evSettings.targetSoc.

data.label: StopTransaction.req (reason=Local)
data.description: §4.8 / §7.36 — StopTransaction.req. The domain transitions Charging → Finishing automatically; autoResetToAvailable then drops the connector to Available once the EV is unplugged.

data.label: Finishing (EV detaching)
data.description: §4.9 C → Finishing: transaction stopped, cable still attached. Real CPs stay here until the EV disconnects.

data.label: EV unplug → Available
data.description: §4.9 A1: cable removed, connector returns to Available and is ready for the next session.
